heading: Loan forgiveness for service in areas of national need
label: 1078–11 Loan forgiveness for service in areas of national need
notes: References in TextSection 9101 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965, referred to in subsec. (b)(5)(A), was amended by and, as so amended, is now section 8101 of the Act and no longer defines “highly qualified”. A reference in this section to the term “highly qualified” as defined in section 9101 of the Act is to be treated as a reference to such term under such section 9101 as in effect on the day before the date of enactment of . children.label: B

children.label: A
children.text: through the holder of the loan, to assume the obligation to repay a qualified loan amount for a loan made, insured, or guaranteed under this part (other than an excepted PLUS loan or an excepted consolidation loan (as such terms are defined in )); andsection 1098e(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: to cancel a qualified loan amount for a loan made under part D of this subchapter (other than an excepted PLUS loan or an excepted consolidation loan).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 3 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to issue such regulations as may be necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: Early childhood educators
children.label: 1 Early childhood educators
children.text: The individual is employed full-time as an early childhood educator.

children.label: A
children.text: as a nurse in a clinical setting; or

children.label: B
children.text: as a member of the nursing faculty at an accredited school of nursing (as those terms are defined in ).section 296 of title 42

children.label: A
children.text: has obtained a baccalaureate or advanced degree in a critical foreign language; and

children.label: i
children.text: in an elementary school or secondary school as a teacher of a critical foreign language;

children.label: ii
children.text: in an agency of the United States Government in a position that regularly requires the use of such critical foreign language; or

children.label: iii
children.text: in an institution of higher education as a faculty member or instructor teaching a critical foreign language.

children.label: A
children.text: a public library that serves a geographic area within which the public schools have a combined average of 30 percent or more of the schools’ total student enrollments composed of children meeting a measure of poverty under ; orsection 6313(a)(5) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: a school that qualifies under for loan cancellation for Perkins loan recipients who teach in such a school.section 1087ee(a)(2)(A) of this title

children.text: has obtained a degree in social work or a related field with a focus on serving children and families; and

children.label: B
children.text: is employed full-time in public or private child welfare services.

children.label: A

children.label: B
children.text: has, at a minimum, a graduate degree in speech-language pathology, audiology, or communication sciences and disorders.

children.label: i
children.text: is licensed by the State or certified by an independent professional regulatory authority;

children.label: ii
children.text: in the absence of such State licensure or certification, possesses national certification in school counseling or a specialty of counseling granted by an independent professional organization; or

children.label: iii
children.text: holds a minimum of a master’s degree in school counseling from a program accredited by the Council for Accreditation of Counseling and Related Educational Programs or the equivalent; and

children.label: B
children.text: is so employed in a school that qualifies under for loan cancellation for Perkins loan recipients who teach in such a school.section 1087ee(a)(2)(A) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: public safety (including as a first responder, firefighter, police officer, or other law enforcement or public safety officer);

children.label: B
children.text: emergency management (including as an emergency medical technician);

children.label: C
children.text: public health (including full-time professionals engaged in health care practitioner occupations and health care support occupations, as such terms are defined by the Bureau of Labor Statistics); or

children.label: D
children.text: public interest legal services (including prosecution, public defense, or legal advocacy in low-income communities at a nonprofit organization).

children.label: A
children.text: is a licensed, certified, or registered dietician who has completed a degree in a relevant field; and

children.label: B
children.text: is employed full-time as a dietician with an agency of the special supplemental nutrition program for women, infants, and children under .section 1786 of title 42

children.label: A
children.text: has received a degree from a medical school at an institution of higher education; and

children.label: i
children.text: requires more than five years of total graduate medical training; and

children.label: ii
children.text: has fewer United States medical school graduate applicants than the total number of positions available in such program or fellowship.

children.label: A
children.text: has not less than a master’s degree in social work, psychology, or psychiatry; and

children.label: B
children.text: is employed full-time providing mental health services to children, adolescents, or veterans.

children.label: i
children.text: has received a degree from an accredited dental school (as accredited by the Commission on Dental Accreditation);

children.label: ii
children.text: has completed residency training in pediatric dentistry, general dentistry, or dental public health; and

children.label: iii
children.text: is employed full-time as a dentist; or

children.label: B
children.text: is employed full-time as a member of the faculty at a program or school accredited by the Commission on Dental Accreditation.

children.heading: STEM employees
children.label: 14 STEM employees
children.text: The individual is employed full-time in applied sciences, technology, engineering, or mathematics.

children.label: A
children.text: is a physical therapist; and

children.label: B
children.text: is employed full-time providing physical therapy services to children, adolescents, or veterans.

children.heading: Superintendents, principals, and other administrators
children.label: 16 Superintendents, principals, and other administrators
children.text: The individual is employed full-time as a school superintendent, principal, or other administrator in a local educational agency, including in an educational service agency, in which 30 percent or more of the schools are schools that qualify under for loan cancellation for Perkins loan recipients who teach in such a school.section 1087ee(a)(2)(A) of this title

children.heading: Occupational therapists
children.label: 17 Occupational therapists
children.text: The individual is an occupational therapist and is employed full-time providing occupational therapy services to children, adolescents, or veterans.

children.label: A
children.text: in a Federal, State, local, or tribal public health agency; or

children.label: B
children.text: in a setting where patients might require health care services, including acute care facilities, ambulatory care facilities, personal residences and other settings located in health professional shortage areas, medically underserved areas, or medically underserved populations, as recognized by the Secretary of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), for each school, academic, or calendar year of full-time employment in an area of national need described in subsection (b) that a borrower completes on or after , the Secretary shall forgive not more than $2,000 of the student loan obligation of the borrower that is outstanding after the completion of each such school, academic, or calendar year of employment, respectively.August 14, 2008

children.heading: Maximum amount
children.label: 2 Maximum amount
children.text: The Secretary shall not forgive more than $10,000 in the aggregate for any borrower under this section, and no borrower shall receive loan forgiveness under this section for more than five years of service.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: d Priority
children.text: The Secretary shall grant loan forgiveness under this section on a first-come, first-served basis, and subject to the availability of appropriations.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: e Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to authorize the refunding of any repayment of a loan.

children.heading: Ineligibility for double benefits
children.label: f Ineligibility for double benefits
children.text: No borrower may, for the same service, receive a reduction of loan obligations under both this section and section 1078–10, 1078–12, 1087e(m), or 1087j of this title.

children.label: A
children.text: has graduated and received an allied health professions degree or certificate from an institution of higher education; and

children.label: B
children.text: is employed with a Federal, State, local or tribal public health agency, or in a setting where patients might require health care services, including acute care facilities, ambulatory care facilities, personal residences and other settings located in health professional shortage areas, medically underserved areas, or medically underserved populations, as recognized by the Secretary of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section such sums as may be necessary for fiscal year 2009 and each of the five succeeding fiscal years to provide loan forgiveness in accordance with this section.
